title: Core Programming Concepts
description: A guided tour through language fundamentals using side-by-side examples for control flow, data structures, modeling, I/O, and problem solving.

content.text: This pathway turns the language-basics snippet library into a structured learning track. Each page groups related concepts so you can compare how the same idea is expressed in C++, Go, Python, TypeScript, Scala 2, and Scala 3.
These lessons are selector-aware: the code examples follow the active language choices in the top bar, so you can study one language or compare two side by side.

items: Start with Foundations & Flow if you are new to syntax and execution order.
Use Types & Modeling to understand how languages represent state, objects, and reusable abstractions.
Visit Data Structures when you want practical examples of how collections and traversal patterns map across ecosystems.
Study Systems & I/O for file access, paths, environment variables, and serialization tasks common in real projects.
Finish with Problem Solving & Reliability to build fluency with recursion, randomness, bit tricks, and defensive programming.

content.text: Learn the building blocks of program execution: working with sequences, branching, repetition, reusable functions, and command-line input.
Explore objects, interfaces, enums, nullable values, and string-heavy data modeling patterns.
Compare the core collection and traversal patterns used for everyday algorithmic thinking.
Practice the parts of programming that interact with the outside world: files, paths, env vars, time, and JSON.
Strengthen your problem-solving toolbox with recursion, error recovery, randomness, hashing, and bit-level reasoning.
